variable_identity.standardized_name_en: Renal replacement therapy fluid removal event

immutable_core.semantic_intent: source-recorded renal replacement therapy fluid-removal event
immutable_core.semantic_definition: A time-stamped event stream retaining source-recorded extracorporeal RRT fluid-removal amount records with source context, standard unit, default-bridge eligibility, and parent support context where available.
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_rrt_fluid_removal_event must preserve event-level RRT extracorporeal fluid-removal amount semantics with source context and must not silently collapse into RRT active state, exact modality episodes, urine output, total output, rate settings, cumulative counters, or fluid-balance summaries.

event_stream_contract.event_time_rule: retain source-faithful charttime or equivalent source event time; no hour smoothing, back-shifting, or episode redistribution in the opening event stream
event_stream_contract.event_value_meaning: source-recorded extracorporeal fluid-removal amount after approved unit normalization, with raw source value and context retained when available
event_stream_contract.unit_rule: retain raw source unit and standardize to mL only when deterministic; rate settings and cumulative counters are outside the opening same-name event numerator
event_stream_contract.context_role_rule: retain source item labels, source unit set, RRT family class, exact modality context, bridge role, and default-balance eligibility so downstream users can distinguish default bridge rows from context-only evidence
event_stream_contract.parent_link_rule: link to approved parent RRT support episodes when available; parent links provide support context but do not replace source fluid-removal event evidence
event_stream_contract.downstream_aggregation_rule: hourly balance augmentation, smoothing, counter differencing, session redistribution, and first-day summaries are separate governed transformations
event_stream_contract.no_row_interpretation: no retained source-recorded RRT fluid-removal event under the approved source rule; this does not prove absence of RRT support or absence of unrecorded fluid removal

canonical_representation.range_role_note: Opening ranges are event-level caution guardrails; raw source values and caution flags should remain visible rather than being silently discarded.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but keep RRT device and dialysis source-context inclusion governed by internal mapping specs.
external_alignment.note: The current MVP identity is governed primarily by database-specific RRT fluid-removal source rows and default/context bridge-role semantics.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing the concept to RRT active state
changing the concept to exact RRT modality episode
changing the concept to hourly fluid-balance summary
mixing urine output or total ICU output into the same event stream
publishing cumulative device counters as source-faithful removal events without a new derived identity
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a reviewed database mapping while preserving event-level RRT fluid-removal meaning
adding a source item with equivalent source-recorded removal amount semantics
tightening caution thresholds while preserving raw source retention

allowed_database_specific_variation: local RRT device and dialysis item identifiers
local source labels and unit spellings
local availability of CRRT, hemodialysis, or peritoneal-dialysis context rows
local parent support episode linkage completeness
database-specific default versus context-only bridge eligibility after review
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing any-RRT active episodes as std_rrt_fluid_removal_event
publishing exact RRT modality episodes as std_rrt_fluid_removal_event
including urine output, total output, generic IO balance, or access-line rows
including rate settings or cumulative machine counters as default source-faithful removal amounts without a governed derived rule
silently converting context-only rows into default balance bridge numerator

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_rrt_fluid_removal_event outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_rrt_fluid_removal_event outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.
